preamble: The shift from the diphthong [ou] (or [oʊ]) to the high back vowel [u] involves monophthongization and vowel raising, often seen in the contrast between standard and regional dialects.

examples.note: Comparison between Standard English [oʊ] and Northern/Scottish [u:] where the diphthong is realized as a long high monophthong.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
